perturbation.original_text: the Receiving Party shall (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise) and (ii) at the option of the Receiving Party, either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy the Confidential Information supplied by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives. Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: the Receiving Party shall (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise). Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause "and (ii) at the option of the Receiving Party, either return to the Disclosing Party or destroy the Confidential Information supplied by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives." from Section 3, the Receiving Party is now only obligated to destroy the information. This creates a contradiction because while the Receiving Party must certify the destruction of materials, it can not also return to the Disclosing party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION UPON REQUEST. Upon the request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall (i) destroy all memoranda, notes, records, drawings, manuals, other documents or materials and any other manifestation (and all copies thereof) pertaining to or arising from the Confidential Information in any form or medium (whether written, typewritten, recorded, electronic or otherwise). Upon the return and/or destruction of such materials, the Receiving Party agrees to certify, in writing, that all of the foregoing materials have been destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows the Receiving Party the option to either return or destroy the Confidential Information. The changed text removes the option to return the information, mandating only destruction. The contradicted text states that the Receiving Party agrees to certify that all the foregoing materials have been either destroyed or surrendered to the Disclosing Party. Since the changed text only allows for destruction, it contradicts the contradicted text which mentions the option of surrendering.

perturbation.original_text: The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party proves: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party free of any obligation of confidence of the Receiving Party to such third party; provided that such third party is not known by the Receiving Party, after reasonable diligence, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the Disclosing Party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party, without obligation of confidentiality, prior to receipt thereof, directly or indirectly, from the Disclosing Party; (d) is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding after all reasonable legal remedies for maintaining such information in confidence have been exhausted, including, but not limited to, giving the Disclosing Party reasonable advance notice of the potential for such disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order concerning such disclosure; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party proves: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party free of any obligation of confidence of the Receiving Party to such third party; provided that such third party is not known by the Receiving Party, after reasonable diligence, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the Disclosing Party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party, without obligation of confidentiality, prior to receipt thereof, directly or indirectly, from the Disclosing Party; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing exception (d) "is requested to be disclosed in a judicial or administrative law, regulation or proceeding after all reasonable legal remedies for maintaining such information in confidence have been exhausted, including, but not limited to, giving the Disclosing Party reasonable advance notice of the potential for such disclosure and allowing the Disclosing Party to seek a protective order concerning such disclosure;" the receiving party may be bound by the confidential agreement even if ordered by court to reveal said confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXCEPTIONS TO CONFIDENTIALITY. The obligation of confidentiality and restriction on use in Section 2 hereof shall not apply to any Confidential Information that the Receiving Party proves: (a) was in the public domain prior to the date of this Agreement or subsequently came into the public domain other than as a result of disclosure by the Receiving Party; (b) was received by the Receiving Party from a third party free of any obligation of confidence of the Receiving Party to such third party; provided that such third party is not known by the Receiving Party, after reasonable diligence, to be bound by a confidentiality agreement with or other obligation of secrecy to the Disclosing Party; (c) was already in the possession of or known to the Receiving Party, without obligation of confidentiality, prior to receipt thereof, directly or indirectly, from the Disclosing Party; or (e) is subsequently and independently developed by employees, consultants or agents of the Receiving Party without reference to the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party disclosed under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained an exception to the confidentiality agreement, allowing disclosure if required by law after exhausting legal remedies. The changed text removes this exception. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, no longer includes the exception. This creates a direct contradiction because the receiving party may now be bound by the confidentiality agreement even when legally compelled to disclose the information, which was not the case in the original agreement, which leads to a strong, direct conflict within the document, because under the changed agreement, they would be in violation of the agreement even if it was requested by a court of law.

perturbation.original_text: Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does not confer any right, license, interest or title in, to or under the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does not confer any right, license, interest or title in, to or under the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the sentence "No license is hereby granted to the Receiving Party, by estoppel or otherwise, under any patent, trademark, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary rights of the Disclosing Party." there is an in-text contradiction that could allow the receiving party to assume that this sentence is not valid under the contract and may use the confidential information for developing their products and services. The rest of section 5 is contradictory due to this ommision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RIGHTS IN CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Except as specifically provided for herein, this Agreement does not confer any right, license, interest or title in, to or under the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information to the Receiving Party. Title to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall remain solely in the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that no license is granted to the Receiving Party under the Disclosing Party's proprietary rights. The changed text omits this crucial sentence, creating a contradiction with the contradicted text, which states that the agreement does not confer any right, license, interest, or title. The omission allows for a potential interpretation that the Receiving Party might have some rights or licenses, conflicting with the intent to keep the Disclosing Party's confidential information protected.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be terminated except following Withdrawal by the Investor pursuant to the terms of the LLC Agreement. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6 until fulfilled and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be terminated except following Withdrawal by the Investor pursuant to the terms of the LLC Agreement. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns." Section 6 creates an in-text contradiction because if the rights are terminated, the assignees of each party will not be bound by the confidentiality agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION. This Agreement may not be terminated except following Withdrawal by the Investor pursuant to the terms of the LLC Agreement. The obligations of confidentiality and limitations on use under this Agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years from the execution of this Agreement; PROVIDED, CONFIDENTIAL Page 29 <Page> HOWEVER, notwithstanding the term of this Agreement, any provisions hereof that by their nature extend beyond such term or termination shall remain in effect beyond such term or termination under this Section 6.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that provisions extending beyond the agreement's term apply to the parties' successors and assigns. The changed text removes "and shall apply to either Party's successors and assigns," thus negating the application of these provisions to successors and assigns. Since the contradicted text (which is the same as the original text) states that the agreement applies to the parties' successors and assigns, the change directly contradicts this by implying that the successors and assigns are no longer bound by the confidentiality agreement after termination. This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the applicability of the agreement's provisions to successors and assigns after termination as it is an omission that goes against another section's instruction, fitting the "Omissions - In Text Contradiction" definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be enforced by any Member of the Company with respect to any information or data regarding such Member disclosed by the Investor m violation of this Agreement. Except as set forth in the preceding sentence, nothing in this Agreement shall provide any benefit to any third party or entitle any third party to any claim, cause of action, remedy or right of any kind, it being the intent of the Parties that this Agreement shall not be construed as a third party beneficiary contract.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be enforced by any Member of the Company with respect to any information or data regarding such Member disclosed by the Investor m violation of this Agreement. Except as set forth in the preceding sentence, nothing in this Agreement shall provide any benefit to any third party or entitle any third party to any claim, cause of action, remedy or right of any kind.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "it being the intent of the Parties that this Agreement shall not be construed as a third party beneficiary contract." the agreement becomes internally contradictory regarding third-party rights. While it states no benefit is provided to third parties, the explicit denial of third-party beneficiary status is missing, potentially opening the door for such claims.
perturbation.contradicted_text: THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARIES. This Agreement may be enforced by any Member of the Company with respect to any information or data regarding such Member disclosed by the Investor m violation of this Agreement. Except as set forth in the preceding sentence, nothing in this Agreement shall provide any benefit to any third party or entitle any third party to any claim, cause of action, remedy or right of any kind.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly stated that the agreement should not be construed as a third-party beneficiary contract. The changed text omits this statement, which creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. By removing this explicit denial, it opens the possibility of a third party claiming beneficiary status, which is contrary to the original intent expressed in the contradicted text (which is the same text as the original text).
